company.name: Eli Lilly
company.subtitle: Mounjaro+Zepbound $40B+ run-rate · Orforglipron oral GLP-1 · $80-83B 2026 guide
company.cashRunway: Highly cash generative ($63.5B 2025 revenue, profitable, growing 45%+ YoY)

market.notes: Mounjaro: $26.1B 2025, Zepbound: $13.5B 2025. Combined Q4: $11.7B ($47B annualized). 60.5% US incretin market share. Zepbound 71% new US obesity Rx. 2026 guide: $80-83B total revenue. Manufacturing capacity +60% H1 2026. Walmart LillyDirect partnership at $349/mo.

us.patientsK.note: CDC NHANES 2023: 42% US adults BMI>=30 = ~110M; ~40M eligible for anti-obesity medications (AOMs) post-insurance criteria.

eu.patientsK.note: WHO Europe 2023: ~150M obese in EU+UK; ~30M eligible for AOMs given stricter HTA BMI/comorbidity gates.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~450M obese; ~50M eligible (China, Japan, AU have growing GLP-1 reimbursement).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Medicare excludes weight-loss indication (Part D statute); commercial coverage expanding post-SELECT CV outcomes.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- NICE/G-BA limited to BMI>=35 + comorbidities; most purchase out-of-pocket.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan PMDA approved, Medicare-equivalent coverage; emerging markets mostly self-pay.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr avg across Mounjaro ($13K), Zepbound ($12K), Wegovy ($16K), Saxenda ($15K); oral GLP-1 pending.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K; post-G-BA negotiation Zepbound/Wegovy in UK, Germany ~50% of US list.

row.priceK.note: $3K blended; Japan PMDA pricing ~$9K, emerging markets ~$1-2K via rebates/tiered pricing.

penPct.note: ~5% peak reflects capacity-constrained launch curve; supply expansion through 2028 unlocks more.

us.reachPct.note: Mounjaro+Zepbound ~2.5M US patients on therapy = ~6% of 40M eligible; growing rapidly, supply-constrained

market.notes: First oral non-peptide GLP-1 RA. FDA approved under National Priority Voucher program Apr 2026. Addresses needle-phobic patients + broader primary care market. Phase 3: significant weight loss. Could expand total GLP-1 market by 2-3x. Peak $10-20B.

eu.patientsK.note: WHO Europe 2023: ~150M obese in EU+UK; ~30M eligible for AOMs given stricter HTA BMI/comorbidity gates.

penPct.note: ~5% peak reflects capacity-constrained launch curve; supply expansion through 2028 unlocks more.

us.reachPct.note: Orforglipron FDA approved Apr 2026; first oral GLP-1 pill, manufacturing advantage

market.notes: Triple agonist (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon). Phase 2: up to 24% weight loss at 48 weeks — best-in-class. Phase 3 ongoing. Could be the Keytruda of obesity if triple mechanism shows metabolic benefits beyond weight loss. Phase 3 for MASH + knee OA + sleep apnea.

us.patientsK.note: CDC NHANES 2023: 42% US adults BMI>=30 = ~110M; ~40M eligible for anti-obesity medications (AOMs) post-insurance criteria.

eu.patientsK.note: WHO Europe 2023: ~150M obese in EU+UK; ~30M eligible for AOMs given stricter HTA BMI/comorbidity gates.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~450M obese; ~50M eligible (China, Japan, AU have growing GLP-1 reimbursement).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Medicare excludes weight-loss indication (Part D statute); commercial coverage expanding post-SELECT CV outcomes.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- NICE/G-BA limited to BMI>=35 + comorbidities; most purchase out-of-pocket.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan PMDA approved, Medicare-equivalent coverage; emerging markets mostly self-pay.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr avg across Mounjaro ($13K), Zepbound ($12K), Wegovy ($16K), Saxenda ($15K); oral GLP-1 pending.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K; post-G-BA negotiation Zepbound/Wegovy in UK, Germany ~50% of US list.

row.priceK.note: $3K blended; Japan PMDA pricing ~$9K, emerging markets ~$1-2K via rebates/tiered pricing.

penPct.note: ~5% peak reflects capacity-constrained launch curve; supply expansion through 2028 unlocks more.

us.reachPct.note: Retatrutide triple agonist Phase 3; highest-efficacy weight loss

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~310K breast/yr; HR+/HER2- ~68% = ~210K incident, ~400K prevalent on-tx including adjuvant ET. Source: NCI SEER 2024.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~240K HR+/HER2- incident; ~450K prevalent on ET +/- CDK4/6. EUROCARE-6 + ESMO 2024 data.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~550K on-tx; China NCC + Japan NCR report HR+/HER2- ~70% of breast mix.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% -- CDK4/6i (Ibrance/Verzenio/Kisqali) NCCN Cat 1 1L; Medicare Part D + commercial broad; adj Verzenio approved 2021.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE + G-BA approved all 3 CDK4/6i; France ASMR III-IV; AI/fulvestrant generic baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Japan J-DPC + China NRDL cover CDK4/6i; EM AI generics dominant (letrozole, anastrozole).

us.priceK.note: Blended $95K: CDK4/6i $160K (40% pts) + Trodelvy/Enhertu-low $180K (8%) + AI+fulvestrant $15K (40%) + Orserdu $220K (5%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$55K blended; EU net ~58% of US list; generic AI floor keeps blend low.

row.priceK.note: ~$20K blended; AI generics <$2K/yr dominate outside developed APAC.

penPct.note: ~58% peak -- CDK4/6i saturated 1L MBC; adj penetration growing 5yr; ESR1-mut Orserdu + ADCs expand late line.

us.reachPct.note: Verzenio HR+ BC adjuvant + mBC; CDK4/6 class leader

us.patientsK.note: NPF: ~8M US psoriasis total; ~1.5M moderate-severe on systemic/biologic; CDC 3% adult prevalence.

eu.patientsK.note: EADV registry: ~2.5M EU27+UK mod-severe on systemic; PASI 75/90/100 targets standard.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~15M mod-severe; Japan high biologic uptake; China NRDL expanding biologic access.

us.wtpPct.note: ~68% -- Medicare + commercial full biologic ladder; IL-23 (Skyrizi/Tremfya) + IL-17 (Cosentyx/Taltz/Bimzelx); Sotyktu TYK2.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~52% -- NICE/G-BA cover full class; adalimumab biosim first-line ~80% share; IL-23/IL-17 2L.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- MTX + phototherapy + adalimumab biosim; Japan J-DPS covers IL-23/IL-17 broadly.

us.priceK.note: Blended $52K: Humira biosim $18K + Skyrizi $85K + Tremfya $75K + Cosentyx $60K + Taltz $60K + Sotyktu $75K; MTX $2K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$26K blended -- adalim biosim ~$8K net + IL-23 ~$35K + IL-17 ~$28K.

penPct.note: ~40% peak -- IL-23/IL-17 shift continues; TYK2 (Sotyktu) oral convenience; biosimilars extend access.

us.reachPct.note: Taltz IL-17A pso+PsA+axSpA; ~8% systemic psoriasis share

us.patientsK.note: Alzheimer's Association 2024: ~6.9M Americans age 65+ with AD; ~2M early-stage eligible for anti-amyloid.

eu.patientsK.note: Alzheimer Europe: ~7.8M prevalent AD EU27+UK; ~1.5M mild-to-moderate amyloid-positive per PET criteria.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~39M global AD; ex-US/EU ~25M, Japan PMDA approved lecanemab (~1M Japan AD).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% - Medicare Part B covers Leqembi/Kisunla post-CMS 2024 decision with CED monitoring; PET/MRI infrastructure limits access.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% - EMA approved Leqembi July 2024 (excl APOE4 homozygotes); NICE rejected, G-BA under review.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% - Japan PMDA covered; China/emerging markets minimal anti-amyloid access, donepezil generics dominate.

us.priceK.note: Blended $28K: Leqembi $26.5K + Kisunla $32K + donepezil/memantine generics ($2K avg, 70% of treated pts).

eu.priceK.note: ~$15K blended; EU anti-amyloid net ~55% of US list, generics widely used.

row.priceK.note: ~$5K blended; Japan near EU pricing, rest generics.

penPct.note: ~12% peak through 2030 - infusion capacity, PET access, and ARIA monitoring cap near-term reach of amyloid-targeting.

us.reachPct.note: Kisunla US ~30-50K pts treated (amyloid-confirmed early AD) of 6M diagnosed; scaling infusion centers

us.patientsK.note: CDC National Diabetes Statistics Report 2024: ~38M US T2D diagnosed; ~25M on pharmacotherapy (metformin + GLP-1 + SGLT2 + insulin).

eu.patientsK.note: IDF Atlas 2023: ~35M EU5+UK T2D; ~22M treated with oral/injectable antihyperglycemics.

row.patientsK.note: IDF global: ~460M T2D ex-US/EU (China 140M, India 100M); ~150M on pharmacotherapy.

us.wtpPct.note: ~90% -- Medicare Part D, Medicaid, commercial cover GLP-1s (Ozempic, Mounjaro) for T2D indication; contrast with obesity exclusion.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~85% -- NICE/G-BA reimburse GLP-1/SGLT2 second-line post-metformin; CV outcomes (LEADER, SUSTAIN-6) drove access.

row.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Japan NHI broad coverage; emerging markets metformin-dominant, GLP-1 access growing.

us.priceK.note: $4K blended: metformin ($50), DPP-4 ($3K), SGLT2 Jardiance/Farxiga ($6K), GLP-1 Ozempic/Mounjaro ($10K), insulin ($2K post-cap).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$2K: GLP-1/SGLT2 ~$4K post-AMNOG/NICE, oral generics dominant.

row.priceK.note: $600 blended: generic metformin/SU dominant, branded GLP-1 ~$3K in Japan/AU.

penPct.note: ~10% peak reflects incremental novel MOA (glucagon/GIP triagonists, oral GLP-1) on top of saturated standard-of-care.

us.reachPct.note: Jardiance SGLT2 T2D+HF; BI partnership, Lilly share ~40% of global

mega_bear.narrative: GLP-1 pricing collapse + competition + orforglipron disappoints
events: ✗ IRA/government pricing pressure collapses GLP-1 margins
✗ Novo Nordisk CagriSema + Amgen MariTide take major share
✗ Orforglipron oral efficacy gap too wide vs injectables — slow uptake
→ Revenue stalls at $70-75B, PE compresses from 50x to 25x

bear.narrative: Growth decelerates + competition intensifies + pricing headwinds
events: ~ Tirzepatide growth slows to 15-20% as market matures
~ Orforglipron approved but 12-15% weight loss (vs 22% injectable)
✗ Retatrutide Phase 3 delayed or mixed results in some indications
→ Revenue $85-90B by 2028, still growing but below expectations

base.narrative: GLP-1 dominance continues + orforglipron expands TAM + retatrutide next wave
events: ✓ Tirzepatide franchise $50B+ by 2028 — dominant in T2D + obesity
✓ Orforglipron expands GLP-1 market 2x (oral access, primary care)
✓ Retatrutide Phase 3 best-in-class weight loss — filing 2028
✓ 2026 guide $80-83B met — strongest growth in large pharma

bull.narrative: Obesity market $150B+ by 2030 — Lilly captures 50%+ — first $2T pharma
events: ✓ Tirzepatide + orforglipron = $70B+ combined peak
✓ Retatrutide 24% weight loss → new obesity standard
✓ Obesity expands to MASH, CKD, sleep apnea, heart failure — all indications
✓ Lilly revenue $120B+ by 2030 — most valuable company in healthcare

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Obesity rewrites human health — Lilly becomes the most important healthcare company on Earth
events: ✓ GLP-1 class proven to reduce CV events, cancer risk, dementia
✓ Total incretin market reaches $200B+ by 2032
✓ Lilly + orforglipron + retatrutide = $100B+ combined franchise
✓ Lilly surpasses $2T market cap, joins Apple/NVIDIA tier

lastData.title: $63.5B 2025 revenue (+45%), 2026 guide $80-83B
points: Zepbound: 71% of new US obesity Rx
Retatrutide: Phase 3 across obesity, MASH, knee OA, sleep apnea

nextCatalyst.title: Orforglipron launch + tirzepatide continued ramp + retatrutide data
thresholds: Orforglipron commercial launch — first oral GLP-1
Q1 2026 earnings: can Lilly maintain $80B+ trajectory?
Manufacturing capacity ramp — supply meeting demand?

risks: $740B market cap at ~50x 2025 PE — any miss devastating
GLP-1 pricing under political/IRA pressure — margins at risk
Novo Nordisk (CagriSema, oral semaglutide 2.0) + Amgen (MariTide) competition
Orforglipron efficacy gap vs injectables — will patients switch?
Retatrutide triple agonist Phase 3 — safety signals possible
Obesity as 'discretionary healthcare' — economic slowdown risk
Concentration risk: >60% of revenue from tirzepatide franchise

catalysts.title: Retatrutide Phase 3 T2D data (TRANSCEND-T2D-1) at ADA
catalysts._rationale: Company confirmed detailed Phase 3 TRANSCEND-T2D-1 results at ADA Scientific Sessions June 5-8, 2026

catalysts.title: Retatrutide additional Phase 3 obesity readouts
catalysts._rationale: Results from three additional retatrutide obesity studies anticipated around mid-year 2026 per BioPharma Dive report
